issue_body:
Build a review workspace that lets creators inspect the assembled long-form episode before export, with a timeline of key sections, warnings for missing captions or empty speaker/template fields, and simple approve/fix actions. This is the next highest-value step because the product can now set up, style, edit, template, contextualize, and export, but creators still need a visible confidence check that the full hour-plus episode is coherent and publish-ready before committing to the final render.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
A creator can open a completed episode in a review screen, see an end-to-end summary of speakers, style/template, audio polish, contextual text improvements, visual moments, captions, and export readiness; any missing or incomplete items are called out with clear creator-facing messages and links/actions to resolve them; the review can be marked approved only when required publish-ready checks pass; the existing verification command `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` passes with tests covering both blocked and approved review states.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The completed steps cover the major creation capabilities in the vision, but a self-serve publishing workflow needs a final quality gate so non-editor creators can trust the assembled long-form result. This advances the visible end-to-end workflow without adding speculative internals or jumping to monetization-style features._
